sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, angle DOUBLE, text_val VARCHAR(255), null_val DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, angle, text_val, null_val) VALUES (1, 0.0, 'Cosine of angle: ', NULL), (2, 1.57, 'Angle: ', 1.234);
SELECT id, cos(angle) AS cosine_value, text_val || cos(angle) AS combined_result FROM test_table WHERE angle IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, text_val || cos(NULLIF(angle, 0)) AS combined_result FROM test_table;
SELECT id, cos(angle) AS cosine_value FROM test_table WHERE text_val || angle::TEXT = 'Angle: 1.57';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, cos(angle) AS cosine_value FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING cos(angle) < 0.5) AS derived;
SELECT A.id, A.text_val || ' has cosine: ' || B.cosine_value FROM (SELECT id, cos(angle) AS cosine_value FROM test_table) AS B JOIN test_table AS A ON A.id = B.id;
SELECT id, cos(angle + 2 * PI()) AS adjusted FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE another_table (field_a INT, field_b VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT 'default');
INSERT INTO another_table (field_a, field_b) VALUES (1, 'Testing'), (2, NULL);
SELECT A.id, A.text_val || COALESCE(B.field_b, 'No value') FROM test_table AS A LEFT JOIN another_table AS B ON A.id = B.field_a;
SELECT id, text_val || ' | Null Value: ' || COALESCE(null_val, 'Empty') FROM test_table ORDER BY cos(angle) DESC;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, angle, text_val, null_val) VALUES (3, -1.57, 'Negative angle value: ', NULL);
SELECT id, angle, cos(angle) AS cosine_value FROM test_table WHERE angle BETWEEN -6.283 AND 6.283;
SELECT id, 'Result: ' || (text_val || cos(angle)::TEXT) AS full_result FROM test_table WHERE cos(angle) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT A.id, A.text_val || ' - Additional Info: ' || cos(B.angle) FROM test_table AS A CROSS JOIN another_table AS B;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, angle FROM test_table WHERE angle IS NOT NULL;
